purpose: Check whether batching several CPU-swapped AdamW parameter shards through torch foreach ops improves the portable AMD swap optimizer path before attempting another full canary run.
read: The foreach-param rung is correct but not a throughput promotion in this ROCm/PyTorch tuple. It improves the cold first step modestly, but warm median latency is flat-to-slower versus torch_param and transient GPU memory rises with foreach batch size. Keep torch_param as the best Python-level portable swap update path. The next performance-relevant rung should move load/update/store into a fused or pipelined HIP/C++ owner rather than expanding PyTorch foreach batching.
validation: Local py_compile passed for torchtitan/components/optimizer.py, tools/dsv4_optimizer_preflight/benchmark_swap_optimizer.py, and tests/unit_tests/test_optimizer_param_groups.py.
Launcher bash -n passed after adding CANARY_OPTIMIZER_SWAP_FOREACH_MAX_PARAMS pass-through.
ROCm container unittest passed on do-sonle5-mi350-gpu: TestOptimizersContainerWithParamGroups.test_swap_opt_states_cpu_foreach_param_matches_torch_param.
